items.title: Tanker Exits Red Sea Dark as Another China Ship Heads to Strait
items.description: A Greek tanker carrying Saudi crude exited the Red Sea with its transponder off, while another Chinese vessel headed toward Bab el-Mandeb as Houthi attacks heightened risks for commercial shipping.

items.title: FTSE 100 Live: UK Stocks Set to Underperform as Oil Pulls Back
China Rushes to Buy East Russian Oil as Middle East Risks Grow
items.description: Chinese buyers are snapping up Russia’s flagship ESPO crude weeks earlier than usual, as heightened risks to Middle East oil flows prompt refiners to secure supplies before they’re needed.

items.title: UK Pub Stocks Fall as Heat Wave Proves Too Hot For Football Fans
items.description: UK pub stocks slumped this week as a sales boost from the World Cup failed to offset the drag from a brutal heat wave and higher costs.

items.title: Dangote Prefers London Over Dubai for Listing of Cement Unit
items.description: Dangote Cement Plc prefers to list its cement business in London because it will be quicker to sell shares on the exchange in the UK than in Dubai, according to Mariya Dangote, a board member in the c

items.title: Global Bonds Are Reeling as Oil Surge Renews Inflation Threat
items.description: Global bonds are being pummeled by the latest resurgence in energy prices, delivering losses to investors who bet the worst of this year’s rout was over and teeing up credibility tests for central ban

items.title: Korean Stocks Sink as Chipmakers Drop on Middle East, China IPO
items.description: South Korean stocks fell as funds cut exposure to chipmakers after overnight losses in US peers, while factoring in a Chinese rival’s imminent share sale and rising Middle East tensions.

items.title: Metals Fluctuate as Middle East Escalation Keep Bulls in Check
items.description: Base metals fluctuated at the end of the week as the threat of an escalating conflict in the Middle East — and more turbulence for the global economy — kept a lid on optimism.

items.title: India’s Markets Shrug Off Student Protests for Now
items.description: Student protests in India’s capital, a political headache for Prime Minister Narendra Modi, aren’t reflecting in the country’s financial markets.

items.title: Wheat Extends July Rally as War Risks Stoke Fears Over Supply
items.description: Wheat resumed its rally after a brief bout of profit-taking, as escalating geopolitical tensions and worsening weather in key producing regions continued to stoke supply concerns.

items.title: Malaysia Sells First Dollar Bonds in Five Years as Fuel Subsidy Bill Rises
items.description: Malaysia raised $1.5 billion in its first dollar bond sale in five years, bolstering funding as the Southeast Asian nation grapples with a fuel subsidy bill that’s likely to more than double from an i

items.title: India’s SEBI Mulls Letting Portfolio Managers Invest Clients’ Money Overseas
items.description: SEBI proposes allowing investment in overseas securities and IPO-bound firms, among others.

items.title: Japan’s Katayama Says to Take Bold FX Steps Decisively as Needed
items.description: Japanese authorities will take bold steps to counter moves in the currency market decisively as needed, and a US Treasury report issued overnight underscores a shared view that excessive currency move

items.title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Falls to Fresh Record Low as Oil Advances
items.description: The Philippine peso fell to a fresh record low as oil prices climbed, boosting pressure on the central bank to intervene as markets eye 62 as the next key level.

items.title: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth
items.description: China’s central bank made its biggest addition of liquidity to the economy in five months via its medium-term lending facility to support growth ahead of its late July Politburo meeting and to accommo

items.title: Gold Extends Decline as Higher Energy Costs Raise Rate-Hike Bets
items.description: Gold extended a retreat as the widening conflict in the Middle East lifted energy prices and increased expectations the US Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

items.title: Stock Slide Extends on AI Jitters, Oil Near $100: Markets Wrap
items.description: A global selloff in technology stocks gained momentum as doubts over returns from billions of dollars spent on artificial intelligence were compounded by a surge in oil prices that rekindled inflation

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
items.description: Brent crude traded near $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea opened a new front in the Middle East conflict, while President Donald Trump threatened to extend US strikes on Ira

items.title: Overseas Buyers In Hot Pursuit of US Crude as Wars Escalate
items.description: US crude is drawing overseas interest from Asian and European refiners as supply concerns spread from the Middle East to the Black Sea.

items.title: India May Allow Portfolio Managers to Short Sell Stocks
items.description: India’s securities regulator has proposed allowing domestic portfolio management service providers to take short positions in equity derivatives for the first time, easing rules for professional money
